mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the federal funds rate over the trailing two quarters raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.9% in April 2017 after multiple 2016-2017 hikes
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during FEDFUNDS uptrends are not statistically lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a full-employment threshold, labor market tightness raises wage and inflation risk expectations, prompting anticipatory equity de-risking that generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.4% in April 2017, below long-run natural rate estimates
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following UNRATE < 4.5% triggers are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the hypothesized negative skew.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that jointly signals robust near-term corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns; neither series alone carries the same predictive power.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month MoM growth > 0.3% AND HOUST 3-month growth > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS vintage 474948 million in April 2017, HOUST vintage 1215k units March 2017
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized consumer and housing strength
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST acceleration episodes fail to produce 20-day returns exceeding single-series baseline models by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and forward order books, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week as markets price in stronger near-term earnings momentum.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 104.0998 (index 2017=100 base) as of March 2017
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC < 0.02) with trailing INDPRO momentum over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI inflation combined with a still-low federal funds rate signals an early-stage reflationary hiking cycle where markets interpret inflation as demand-driven rather than stagflationary, producing positive S&P 500 returns; the same inflation signal turns negative once FEDFUNDS approaches neutral, since it then implies terminal-rate risk.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY rising for 3+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage 244.193 April 2017 with FEDFUNDS at 0.9%
regime_hint: early-stage monetary tightening cycle with rate still below neutral
failure_condition: The direction of the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction effect fails to flip sign when FEDFUNDS crosses above 2.5% in later out-of-sample periods, or 60-day returns show no regime-dependent divergence (interaction coefficient not significant at p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year contraction in industrial production signals a manufacturing-led slowdown that precedes broader earnings deterioration, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once the contraction threshold is crossed.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 104.0998 March 2017, requiring YoY comparison for trigger evaluation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO YoY < 0% triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in housing starts over multiple quarters reflects durable consumer confidence and credit availability, feeding through construction and durable-goods spending into corporate earnings growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no significant positive association (Rank IC < 0.03) with trailing 6-month HOUST trend across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A Sahm-rule-style joint signal, where the 3-month moving average of unemployment rises meaningfully above its 12-month low while payroll growth simultaneously decelerates, indicates an emerging labor market inflection that markets have not yet priced, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; neither series alone reliably triggers this effect.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month MA rises > 0.3pp above trailing 12-month low AND PAYEMS 3-month average monthly gain falls below prior 12-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE 4.4% and PAYEMS 146175k (revised) as of April 2017
regime_hint: labor market inflection near cycle peak
failure_condition: Joint UNRATE/PAYEMS trigger episodes fail to produce negative 20-day returns more often than the unconditional base rate (65.5% positive), or interaction term is not significant at p < 0.10 in walk-forward testing.
